title: Type-safety verification and the end-to-end example workflow
purpose: Prove the cluster's defining property — compile-time type safety — and demonstrate the whole surface composing. Assert representative invalid compositions are rejected by gleam build, and write a typed end-to-end example workflow exercising run/now/sleep/receive/spawn/all that compiles and passes under the harness.
task: Add type-safety verification: document representative invalid compositions (wrong activity input type, wrong signal payload type, wrong query return type) as compile-fail expectations and verify they are rejected by gleam build (e.g. via a documented compile-fail fixture set or an inline negative-test approach). Write an end-to-end typed example workflow that uses run, now, sleep, receive, spawn, and all, and a harness test that drives it to a typed result.

requirements.title: Compile-time type-safety verification
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL document representative invalid compositions as compile-fail expectations and verify they are rejected by gleam build: a wrong activity input type, a wrong signal payload type, and a wrong query return type.
acceptance: At least three invalid compositions are captured: (a) passing a value of the wrong type as an activity input, (b) receiving a SignalRef(a) into a context expecting payload b, (c) a query handler whose returned type does not match its registered type.
Each is documented (in a clearly marked fixture or a doc-tested comment block) with the expected gleam build rejection, and the positive counterpart compiles.

The verification approach is stated in the brief's notes so a reviewer can reproduce the compile-fail (e.g. a committed fixture compiled in a separate check, or documented expected-error snippets) — the point is that the static guarantee is demonstrated, not merely asserted in prose.

requirements.title: End-to-end typed example workflow
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide an end-to-end typed example workflow that exercises run, now, sleep, receive, spawn, and all, compiling and passing under the test harness.
acceptance: The example is a plain Gleam workflow function fn(input) -> Result(output, error) registered with workflow.define, using typed activities (run), workflow.now, a sleep, a typed receive, a child spawn, and an all fan-out — demonstrating the full surface composes with static types.
A harness test (AF-011) drives the example: mocks its activities and child, advances the clock past its sleep, sends its signal, and asserts the typed output.
The example doubles as the canonical documentation snippet for the package (referenced from aion_flow.gleam's module doc).

boundaries: SHALL NOT add new primitives — this brief verifies and demonstrates the surface built in AF-001 through AF-011.
SHALL NOT require a running engine — the example runs under the pure-Gleam harness.
SHALL NOT weaken any type signature to make the example compile; if a composition needs a type that does not exist, that is a gap to raise, not to paper over.
verification: gleam build passes for the package and the positive example.
gleam test passes for the end-to-end example under the harness.
The documented invalid compositions are confirmed rejected by gleam build; the positive counterparts compile.
No panic/todo; no type signature weakened to force compilation.
